message: git_safety_guard-49sj Skip command strip on unknown options

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-3zi","title":"[PACK] secrets.onepassword - 1Password CLI Pack","description":"# 1Password CLI Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for 1Password CLI (op) operations.

## Destructive Patterns
- `op item delete` - Deletes items
- `op vault delete` - Deletes vaults
- `op document delete` - Deletes documents
- `op group delete` - Deletes groups
- `op user delete` - Deletes users
- `op connect token delete` - Revokes tokens

## Safe Patterns
- `op item get`, `op item list`
- `op vault get`, `op vault list`
- `op read`
- `op whoami`, `op account get`

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: secrets.* (git_safety_guard-a8v)
- Keywords: op
- Consider: op inject for secret injection

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All delete operations blocked
- [ ] Read operations allowed
- [ ] Tests cover common 1Password workflows","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:31:03.54068136-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T17:37:08.463172668-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T17:37:08.463172668-05:00","close_reason":"Completed: added secrets.onepassword pack + tests and registered in pack registry. Docs update delegated to mbq7.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-3zi","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:34.547565782-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-3zi","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:34.589051117-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-3zi","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:34.630680253-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-3zi","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:34.672848874-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-3zi","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:34.717729157-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-3zi","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:34.759421932-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-3zi","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:09.948474746-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-3zi","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:09.993176789-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-484","title":"Coverage: no-mock unit tests + gating","description":"## Objective

Drive unit/integration test coverage toward **full functional coverage** while avoiding mocks/fakes. Emphasize real evaluator/packs behavior so failures reflect production reality.

## Current notes

- Mocks exist today (e.g., mock patterns in `src/evaluator.rs` tests).
- Coverage tooling exists (`scripts/coverage.sh`, CI `cargo llvm-cov`), but threshold is informational only.

## Deliverables

- Coverage audit and mock inventory with concrete replacement plan.
- Replacement of mock-based tests with real fixtures for evaluator/pack paths.
- CI gating with explicit thresholds once baseline is known.

## Acceptance criteria

- No mock/fake pattern types in core evaluator tests unless explicitly justified.
- Coverage thresholds enforced in CI with a documented baseline.
- Coverage report differentiates unit vs integration coverage (where possible).

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-49s","title":"Implement heredoc syntax detection (regex quick-check)","description":"# Implement Heredoc Syntax Detection (Regex Quick-Check)

## Objective

Implement a fast regex-based detector that identifies commands potentially containing heredoc content.

## Background

Before we can scan heredoc content, we need to detect its presence. This is a two-stage approach:

1. Quick regex check (this task) - fast, may have false positives
2. Full extraction (separate task) - accurate, only runs if quick check triggers

## Implementation Details

### Quick-Check Patterns

The quick check should match common heredoc indicators:

```rust
// Patterns to detect heredoc presence
static HEREDOC_QUICK_PATTERNS: &[&str] = &[
    r"<<[-~]?\s*['\"]?\w+['\"]?",  // << EOF, <<- 'EOF', <<~ "END"
    r"<<<\s*['\"]",                 // <<< "here string"
    r"-c\s+['\"]",                  // python -c "code", bash -c 'code'
    r"-e\s+['\"]",                  // node -e "code", perl -e 'code'
];
```

### Integration Point

Add to the main processing pipeline after quick-reject but before pattern matching:

```rust
fn process_command(cmd: &str) -> Decision {
    // Existing quick-reject (no git/rm)
    if quick_reject(cmd) && global_quick_reject(cmd) {
        return Decision::Allow;
    }

    // NEW: Check for potential heredoc content
    if has_potential_heredoc(cmd) {
        if let Some(reason) = scan_heredoc_content(cmd) {
            return Decision::Deny(reason);
        }
    }

    // Continue with existing pattern matching...
}
```

### Performance Requirements

- Quick check must complete in < 0.5ms
- Should not allocate on the hot path
- Use lazy-compiled static patterns

### False Positive Tolerance

- Quick check can have false positives (triggers full scan unnecessarily)
- Quick check must NOT have false negatives (miss actual heredocs)
- Full scan will handle false positives gracefully

## Files to Modify

- src/packs/mod.rs or new src/heredoc/mod.rs
- src/main.rs (pipeline integration)

## Testing

- Test each heredoc variant is detected
- Test non-heredoc commands don't trigger
- Benchmark quick check performance

## Dependencies

- Design: heredoc detection strategy (git_safety_guard-o15)
- ADR approved (git_safety_guard-5ib)

## Acceptance Criteria

- All heredoc variants from design doc are detected by the quick-check.
- Quick check adds < 0.5ms to non-heredoc commands (validate via benchmark once perf harness exists).
- Zero false negatives in the tier-1 trigger test suite.

+{"id":"git_safety_guard-49sj","title":"Normalize: avoid stripping command wrapper on unknown options","description":"Unknown options for  builtin indicate non-standard usage; treat as non-wrapper to avoid false positives when trailing args are not executed.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-12T13:07:08.123938926-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-12T13:08:31.427182304-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-12T13:08:31.427182304-05:00","close_reason":"Completed"}
 {"id":"git_safety_guard-4a9","title":"[PACK] backup.velero - Velero Pack","description":"# Velero Kubernetes Backup Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for Velero Kubernetes backup operations.

## Destructive Patterns
- `velero backup delete`
- `velero schedule delete`
- `velero restore delete`
- `velero backup-location delete`
- `velero snapshot-location delete`
- `velero uninstall`

## Safe Patterns
- `velero backup get`
- `velero backup describe`
- `velero backup logs`
- `velero backup create`
- `velero schedule get`
- `velero restore create`
- `velero version`

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: backup.* (git_safety_guard-alc)
- Keywords: velero
- Note: Kubernetes-specific backup solution

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Backup deletion blocked
- [ ] Schedule deletion blocked
- [ ] Location deletion blocked
- [ ] Backup creation allowed
- [ ] Tests cover common Velero operations","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T14:32:53.777359245-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T18:46:44.92387398-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-10T18:46:44.92387398-05:00","close_reason":"Completed","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-4a9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ikf2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:30.646793098-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-4a9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-i79","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:30.687266389-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-4a9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-ct5s","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:30.725849073-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-4a9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-jdb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:30.766934346-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-4a9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-e7j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:30.807843487-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-4a9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-l968","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:07:30.847686993-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-4a9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-qxc7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:08.470626554-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"git_safety_guard-4a9","depends_on_id":"git_safety_guard-6ozg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-09T17:47:08.518606239-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

- `gcloud apigee environments list`

 {"id":"git_safety_guard-4km","title":"[PACK] email.mailgun - Mailgun Pack","description":"# Mailgun Email Pack

## Scope
Implement pattern matching for Mailgun API operations.

## Destructive Patterns
### API Operations
- Domain deletion: `DELETE /v3/domains/{domain}`
- Route deletion: `DELETE /v3/routes/{id}`
- Credential deletion
- Mailing list deletion: `DELETE /v3/lists/{address}`
- Template deletion
- Webhook deletion

## Safe Patterns
- `GET /v3/domains`
- `GET /v3/routes`
- `GET /v3/stats`
- Domain listing
- Log retrieval
- Email sending (POST)

## Implementation Notes
- Parent category: email.* (git_safety_guard-62v)
- Keywords: mailgun
- Note: REST API based

## Testing Requirements
- [ ] Unit tests for domain deletion
- [ ] Unit tests for route deletion
- [ ] Unit tests for mailing list deletion
- [ ] E2E test: `scripts/e2e_tests/email_mailgun.txt`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Domain deletion blocked
- [ ] Route deletion blocked
- [ ] List operations allowed","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-09T15:12:24.159683687-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-11T04:48:23.030746704-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-11T04:48:23.030746704-05:00","close_reason":"Implemented email.mailgun pack with 8 destructive patterns.
